Gender Pay Gap Report

Kaplan International — Aspect International Language Academies Ltd

Our commitment to gender pay equality

Kaplan International is committed to creating an inclusive working environment where all employees are valued and rewarded fairly. We believe in transparency around pay and are committed to reducing the gender pay gap across our organisation.

What is the gender pay gap?

The gender pay gap is a measure of the difference between the average earnings of men and women across an organisation. It is different from equal pay — which is about ensuring that men and women receive the same pay for the same work. The gender pay gap reflects broader structural factors including the distribution of men and women across different roles and levels of seniority within the organisation.

About our workforce

As an international education business, Kaplan International employs a diverse workforce across a wide range of roles — from teachers and academic managers to operations, technology, sales, marketing, finance and human resources professionals. Our workforce spans multiple countries and cultures, reflecting the international student communities we serve.

Reporting entity

Our actions

We are committed to a range of actions to help close the gender pay gap over time:

  • Structured pay bands — ensuring that pay decisions are based on transparent criteria linked to role, experience and performance.
  • Flexible working — offering flexible and hybrid working arrangements to support employees at all stages of their careers.
  • Parental leave — providing enhanced maternity, paternity and shared parental leave to support families.
  • Recruitment practices — reviewing job descriptions and recruitment processes to remove unconscious bias and attract diverse talent.
  • Leadership development — investing in programmes that support all employees, including women, to progress into senior roles.

Confirmation of accuracy

The information and data reported is accurate and has been prepared in accordance with the Equality Act 2010 (Gender Pay Gap Information) Regulations 2017.

Note: For the full statutory gender pay gap data figures, please refer to our submission on the Government's gender pay gap reporting service at gender-pay-gap.service.gov.uk .
